mnemonics.title: Water-Borne Disease Types
mnemonics.content: Remember as 'CHAD GAT': Cholera, Hepatitis, Amoebiasis, Dysentery, Giardiasis, Amoebiasis, Typhoid - all fecal-oral transmission.

mnemonics.title: Cholera Symptoms
mnemonics.content: 'RICE' - Rapid onset, Intense rice-water stools, Cramping (abdominal), Extreme dehydration.

mnemonics.title: Vector Mosquitoes
mnemonics.content: 'AAAC' - Aedes (Aedes aegypti: Dengue), Anopheles (Malaria), Anopheles/Culex (Filariasis), Culex (Japanese Encephalitis).

acronyms.usage: Treatment for dehydration caused by cholera and diarrhea - mixture of salt, sugar, and clean water.
Global health body that reports statistics on water-related diseases and sets drinking water guidelines.
WHO/UNICEF joint program to improve access to clean water and prevent water-borne diseases.
Memory aid for preventing fecal-oral transmission - wash hands, use clean water, maintain hygiene.

visualTips.visualization: Draw two columns - LEFT: Water-Borne (contaminated H2O → mouth → gut), RIGHT: Water-Induced (mosquito breeding in water → bite → disease). Connect each disease to appropriate column.
Create a table with Disease | Causative Agent | Transmission Type | Symptom Key - color code bacterial (red), viral (blue), parasitic (green).
Draw mosquito silhouettes - Aedes (striped legs), Anopheles (angled body when resting), Culex (parallel body). Label breeding sites and diseases each transmits.

analogies.analogy: Like a leaky water balloon - body loses water and salts rapidly through 'rice-water' diarrhea, needing ORS to 'refill' like adding water back to the balloon.
Think of it as 'circular contamination' - what goes in (contaminated water) eventually comes out (feces with pathogens), which can contaminate more water if hygiene fails.
Stagnant water containers are like 'mosquito apartments' - Aedes rents in tire/flower pot, Anopheles in pond 'suburbs', Culex in dirty water 'slums'.
